title: DIY Wedding Favor: Mini Onion‑Jam Jars Made with Stainless Steel Slicer
meta_description: Create a gourmet onion jam wedding favor your guests will love. Learn how to slice perfectly uniform onions safely with our favorite kitchen tool.
html:
There is something incredibly thoughtful about a homemade gift that guests can actually use. While candles and succulents are classic, a culinary gift feels personal and high-end. One of my favorite ideas for a sophisticated, budget-friendly treat is a savory onion jam wedding favor. It’s the perfect condiment for charcuterie boards, burgers, or morning toast, and it looks beautiful tucked into a mini mason jar with a bit of twine.

The secret to a professional-grade onion jam isn't just the slow-cooking process; it's the prep. If your onion slices are uneven—some thick, some paper-thin—they won't caramelize at the same rate. You'll end up with some burnt bits and some raw chunks. To get that consistent, jammy texture, you need precision slicing without spending three hours at the cutting board.

The Secret to Uniform Slicing

When you're prepping dozens of jars for a wedding, the repetitive motion of slicing onions can get tedious and, frankly, a bit dangerous. This is where a specialized tool makes a massive difference. I highly recommend using the Onion Holder & Slicing Guide (2 PCS) to keep your fingers away from the blade and your slices perfectly consistent.

These stainless steel prongs slide directly into the onion, holding it steady as you glide your knife through. Because the guide maintains a set distance between the prongs, every single slice comes out the same thickness. This is a game-changer for an onion jam wedding favor because uniform slices ensure a smooth, cohesive jam rather than a chunky relish.

Ingredients for Gourmet Onion Jam

To make a batch that feels "wedding-ready," you want a balance of sweet, savory, and acidic. Here is what you'll need for a standard batch (which you can scale up based on your guest list):

    n
  • 5 lbs of yellow or sweet onions (sliced uniformly using your slicing guide)
  • 1/2 cup unsalted butter
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1/2 cup balsamic vinegar
  • 1 tablespoon salt
  • 1 teaspoon black pepper
  • A pinch of red pepper flakes (optional, for a subtle kick)

Step-by-Step: Crafting Your Onion Jam Wedding Favor

Start by prepping your onions. Use the Onion Holder & Slicing Guide (2 PCS) to slice all your onions into thin, half-moon shapes. Having the stainless steel prongs hold the vegetable steady means you can work faster and safer, which is critical when you're processing pounds of produce.

Melt the butter in a large, heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and a pinch of salt. Cook them slowly, stirring occasionally, until they soften and turn a golden brown. This caramelization process is where the flavor lives, so don't rush it.

Once the onions are golden, stir in the brown sugar, balsamic vinegar, and seasonings. Lower the heat to a simmer and cook for another 30 to 45 minutes. The mixture should thicken and become glossy, resembling a thick jam. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly before jarring.

Packaging and Presentation

The presentation is what turns a condiment into a wedding favor. Use 2-ounce or 4-ounce mini mason jars. Sterilize the jars in boiling water first to ensure the jam stays fresh. Spoon the jam in, leaving a small amount of headspace at the top.

For a rustic look, cover the lid with a small circle of brown kraft paper or a piece of linen fabric, secured with a piece of jute twine. Attach a small handwritten tag that says "Sweet & Savory" or "A Perfect Match," along with a brief serving suggestion (like "Pairs great with brie!").

Versatility Beyond the Wedding

One of the things I love about the Onion Holder & Slicing Guide (2 PCS) is that it doesn't just sit in the drawer after the wedding favors are done. Because it's made of durable stainless steel, it's a workhorse in the kitchen. I use mine for slicing tomatoes for sandwiches or potatoes for home fries, ensuring I never cut my fingertips.

Additionally, the sturdy design allows it to double as a meat tenderizer. If you're prepping a roast or chicken breasts, you can use the prongs to create channels in the meat for marinades to penetrate deeper, making it a truly multi-purpose tool for any home cook.

FAQ

How long does homemade onion jam last?

When stored in airtight jars in the refrigerator, homemade onion jam typically lasts for 3 to 4 weeks. If you are making these far in advance of the wedding, you can freeze them for up to 3 months.

Can I use red onions instead of yellow?

Absolutely. Red onions provide a slightly sweeter flavor and a beautiful deep purple hue to the final jam. The slicing process remains exactly the same using the slicing guide.

Is the slicing guide dishwasher safe?

Yes, since the Onion Holder & Slicing Guide (2 PCS) is made of stainless steel, it is dishwasher safe. Just be sure to rinse any onion residue off before popping them in the rack.
